clusters.justification: These terms map onto subcortical and paralimbic co-activation patterns, such as the amygdala and anterior insula, which broadly mediate emotional reactivity, valence, and mood.
These concepts share robust frontostriatal and orbitofrontal co-activations dedicated to computing expected utility, processing primary reinforcers, and driving goal-directed behavior.

These terms converge on the midline structural hubs of the brain's internally-directed processing systems, which support abstract, constructed representations of self, others, and interpersonal narratives.
